steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'In 2016, the number of cellular and molecular biology topic submissions was the same as the number of animal science topic submissions.': This compares two unrelated topics in 2016 and says nothing about medicine and health submissions in 2019. ✗
  • Choice B — 'In 2019, there were more physics and space science topic submissions than there were medicine and health topic submissions.': If physics and space science exceeded medicine and health in 2019, that would undercut rather than support the claim. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The lowest number of animal science topic submissions in a year was approximately 95 in 2016.': Animal science data is irrelevant to a claim about medicine and health peaking in 2019. ✗
  • Choice D — 'The highest number of medicine and health topic submissions during the period shown is approximately 285 in 2019.': This directly identifies the 2019 medicine and health bar as the highest across all years shown, which is exactly what the claim requires. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice D is the only option that reads the medicine and health data and shows 2019 was the peak year, providing direct evidence for the student's claim. That matches choice D — D is correct.
